problem_statement: Whole-slide pathology previews intermittently miss their review target during an overnight window, delaying report assembly. Aggregate host signals suggest an execution-environment effect, but current records do not tie that mechanism to individual failed jobs. Determine the justified next action without touching clinical source images.

observations.statement: Matched-concurrency probes retained from six weeks before and two weeks after the alert show the same 3.1 to 3.4 percent long-tail rate on the shared worker pool. Higher nightly volume made the existing tail cross the operational alert threshold.
Long jobs cluster between 01:05 and 01:40, beginning shortly after an archival compression process starts on some of the same hosts and ending as its nightly queue drains.

The report assembler waits for preview completion and then marks the study delayed; it reports no malformed tile or missing object, making the report status a downstream effect rather than a mechanism observation.

Source image hashes, tile counts, stain profiles, and decode validation match successful previews from the same scanners. Reprocessing sampled sources on a dedicated diagnostic host completes within target.
observations.provenance: read-only source validation and diagnostic replay

observations.statement: Small preview jobs on shared hosts remain fast during the window, and whole-slide jobs outside the window complete normally through the same queue, storage path, and worker release.

One-minute host metrics show scheduler wait and memory-pressure stalls rising on shared hosts during the compression window, while storage latency, network errors, worker heap pauses, and queue broker latency remain ordinary.

Worker images, decoder packages, process limits, placement declarations, and transformation recipes are unchanged across the retained period. No application restart aligns consistently with a long job.

Current traces identify the worker process and total job duration but omit host run-queue delay and pressure-stall time for that process. Host aggregation cannot show whether any sampled failed job was runnable but unscheduled.

A bounded next measurement can collect per-process scheduler delay, pressure-stall counters, host identity, and job phase for the next 50 preview jobs, then compare them with 50 matched jobs on the dedicated pool. Sampling is read-only, excludes image content, and has a fixed overhead budget.
